context: Tests whether the agent correctly handles ogen-generated clients where a single endpoint can return multiple success status codes (200 and 201). The key pattern: always type-switch all success variants, never single type-assert. Derived from incident da4135cf where 'relations create' returned a false error on HTTP 201.

checklist.name: Type switch over single assertion
checklist.description: Response handling uses a type switch (switch res := res.(type)) rather than a single type assertion (res.(*CreateEntryRelationOK)) to handle the response

checklist.name: Both 200 and 201 variants handled
checklist.description: The type switch (or equivalent) includes cases for both *CreateEntryRelationOK and *CreateEntryRelationCreated

checklist.name: No false error on 201
checklist.description: The implementation does not produce an error when the server returns HTTP 201 — i.e., 201 is treated as a success path, not an unexpected response

checklist.name: Error path for unrecognized response
checklist.description: The default/fallthrough case in the switch (or equivalent) returns an error for truly unexpected response types

checklist.name: CLI flags defined correctly
checklist.description: The command defines --entry-id, --target-id, and --relation flags as required string flags

checklist.name: Output marshaled as JSON
checklist.description: The created relation data is marshaled to JSON and printed to stdout

checklist.name: Non-zero exit on error
checklist.description: The function returns an error (or calls os.Exit(1)) on failure cases, not just prints a message
